ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "uniq_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for data extraction */,
  "crawl_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job was crawled */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job position */,
  "job_salary" TEXT /* Salary offered for the job */,
  "experience_required" TEXT /* Experience required for the job */,
  "key_skills" TEXT /* Key skills necessary for the job */,
  "role_category" TEXT /* Role category of the job */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the job is based */,
  "functional_area" TEXT /* Functional area of the job */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry in which the job is categorized */,
  "employer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employer who posted the job */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job */,
  "salary_min" INTEGER /* Minimum salary range */,
  "salary_max" INTEGER /* Maximum salary range */,
  "is_remote" INTEGER /* Indicates if the job is remote */,
  "posting_date" TEXT /* Date when the job was posted */,
  "closing_date" TEXT /* Date when the job posting will close */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views the job posting has received */,
  "applications" INTEGER /* Number of applications received for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_employer_id FOREIGN KEY ("employer_id") REFERENCES employers ("employer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "candidates" (
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each candidate */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the candidate */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the candidate */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the candidate */,
  "resume_link" TEXT /* Link to the candidate's resume */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date when the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the application (e.g., applied, interview, hired) */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Candidate's date of birth */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Candidate's gender */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education */,
  "current_employer" TEXT /* Current employer (if applicable) */,
  "current_position" TEXT /* Current position (if applicable) */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Total years of experience */,
  "preferred_location" TEXT /* Preferred location for the job */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the candidate is actively looking for a job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("candidate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the candidate who applied */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting applied to */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date when the application was made */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the application */,
  "interview_date" TEXT /* Date of the scheduled interview */,
  "interviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interviewer */,
  "interview_notes" TEXT /* Notes from the interview */,
  "offer_date" TEXT /* Date when the offer was made */,
  "offer_status" TEXT /* Status of the offer (e.g., accepted, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_candidate_id FOREIGN KEY ("candidate_id") REFERENCES candidates ("candidate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_interviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("interviewer_id") REFERENCES employers ("employer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employers" (
  "employer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employer */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company posting the job */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the company */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company website URL */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the company */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry in which the company operates */,
  "company_size" TEXT /* Size of the company (e.g., small, medium, large) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the employer account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., recruiter, hiring manager) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON array of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
